published_prior_art.cite: DingLu2017 and JiangYangDing2022: principal-score point identification under principal ignorability
NguyenStuartScharfsteinOgburn2024 plus DornGuoKallus2022: binary/one-pair odds-ratio sensitivity geometry

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_ordinal_late_partial_defiers_v1 Conjecture 1: shared ordinal middle path makes pairwise bounds strictly too wide

structure_flags.label: Bibliography
structure_flags.one_line: The bibitem ForastiereMealliVanderWeele2018 has the wrong authors for the Biometrika 2018 paper; the verified authors are Forastiere, Mattei, and Ding.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: §5 says the closest documented ThmSmith proposals are q1/Q2 panel entries, but pid_ordinal_late_partial_defiers_v1 is a closer repo analogue with the same shared-middle ordinal compatibility mechanism, though for IV response paths rather than principal-stratum posteriors.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Correct the Forastiere citation key and bibliography metadata to Forastiere-Mattei-Ding 2018.
Add an explicit repo-prior-art paragraph distinguishing this posterior-chain LP from pid_ordinal_late_partial_defiers_v1's ordinal IV response-path compatibility facet.
Add a minimal K=3 nondegenerate finite-support witness showing strict containment of the edgewise binary relaxation before claiming an open-set gap.
State a variant where the known principal-score vector is replaced by an externally identified or sensitivity-indexed principal-score polytope.

recommended_next_step: Patch the bibliography and repo comparison, then rerun novelty review focused on whether the principal-stratum posterior-chain mechanism is more than an ordinal-compatibility transplant.
